behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is usually in state handoff (retry counter -> backoff calculator), not just math. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed; fake clock + stub transport reproduces it. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): call `computeBackoff(attempt)` for attempts 1..N; catches formula bugs; misses counter reset/wiring bugs. Current(Integration): client with stub API that returns rate-limit repeatedly, fake timers, assert scheduled delays [d,2d,4d,...,max]; catches wiring/state bugs. High(System): real HTTP server + real sleeps; catches little extra for this behavior, mainly timing flake.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry module + backoff policy + request executor with a stubbed rate-limit response and fake timers; trigger consecutive failures and assert each scheduled delay doubles and clamps at configured max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior spans response classifier + retry policy. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process stub HTTP response objects; real network not required. Rejected higher level: behavior text is about identifying response and computing wait, not real socket behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): classifier function on fixture payloads; catches parsing bugs; misses integration between classifier and scheduler. Current(Integration): stub transport returns 429 + headers, assert retry branch taken and computed delay matches policy/header precedence. High(System): real HTTP server; mostly redundant here.
behaviors.test_description: Use composed client modules with a stub transport returning representative rate-limit responses (e.g., 429 and retry-after metadata); assert retry is triggered and computed wait equals expected policy output.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration across timer, request replay, and response propagation. REAL-INFRA: fake timers + stub transport are sufficient; real network is not required by behavior text. Rejected System: no requirement about real HTTP stack quirks, only resend semantics. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): test resend helper with copied request object; catches clone bugs; misses end-to-end transparent return path. Current(Integration): first call gets rate-limit, second returns success, assert identical request payload resent and final `call()` resolves with success value. High(System): real server adds little beyond integration for this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Configure transport stub to fail once with rate-limit then succeed; advance fake clock past wait; assert second attempt payload/method/path/body match the original call and returned value is passed back without extra wrapper artifacts.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: typed error comes from retry-loop termination + error mapping, a module boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network dependency; repeated stubbed failures suffice. Rejected higher level: behavior explicitly concerns error type after internal retry count, not real infrastructure behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): mapper converts terminal condition to `QuotaExhaustedError`; catches mapping bug; misses loop stopping condition. Current(Integration): force max+1 rate-limit responses, assert promise rejects with `QuotaExhaustedError` and not generic error. High(System): real HTTP unnecessary for this contract.
behaviors.test_description: Run client with maxRetries=N and transport always returning rate-limit; assert exactly terminal failure produces `QuotaExhaustedError` instance/type tag and preserves expected metadata (attempt count/quota context if exposed).

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears in interaction of retry controller + quota tracker + termination guard. REAL-INFRA: deterministic fake timers and stub transport can expose infinite-retry logic; real OS/network not needed. Rejected System: behavior is control-flow termination, not infrastructure fidelity. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): termination predicate function; catches off-by-one; misses chain-level loop interactions. Current(Integration): always rate-limit response, advance fake time, assert attempts bounded and final rejection occurs. High(System): real sleeps/network increase flakiness without extra failure coverage.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full retry path with quota-tracker enabled and perpetual rate-limit responses; advance fake clock until completion and assert call settles (rejects) within bounded virtual time and attempt count <= configured limit.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is shared-state coordination across concurrent callers and quota module handoff. REAL-INFRA: behavior text says "same process"; in-process concurrent test reproduces it without real network. Rejected System by text: no cross-process/distributed coordination required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): quota bucket object decrements correctly sequentially; catches arithmetic bugs; misses race/async interleaving. Current(Integration): multiple parallel `call()` invocations through real client wiring, assert global quota consumption reflects all callers and not per-instance isolation. High(System): multi-process/network adds scope beyond stated behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate shared client/quota manager, fire concurrent async calls (with controlled scheduler/stub transport), and assert aggregate token usage and throttling decisions are based on one shared bucket across callers.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug emerges at queue + dispatcher + retry wakeup boundary, not a single function only. REAL-INFRA: fake timers and in-process queue executor are enough; real OS/network not required. Rejected System: behavior is logical dispatch order, not transport-level nondeterminism. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): queue data structure FIFO test; catches container bug; misses dispatcher scheduling interactions. Current(Integration): enqueue multiple blocked calls, release capacity, assert transport send sequence equals submission order. High(System): real HTTP offers little extra for FIFO contract.
behaviors.test_description: Create contention so several calls wait (quota exhausted), then restore capacity stepwise; assert actual send order recorded by transport stub matches original submission order exactly.
